ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) ProxyLogon webshell drop — w3wp.exe writing .aspx/.ashx into Exchange OWA/ECP paths
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM tradecraft: post-CVE-2021-26855 SSRF, the IIS worker hosting OWA (w3wp.exe) writes a webshell (China Chopper / 'tabBackdoor' / 'orange.aspx' variants) into Exchange virtual directories or aspnet_client. This is the file-system half of Operation Exchange Marauder and remains in the playbook against Exchange 2013/2016/2019, Citrix, and Ivanti edges Silk Typhoon pivots through.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_path values(Filesystem.file_hash) as file_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name="w3wp.exe" AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ashx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.asmx") AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\owa\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\ecp\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ClientAccess\\Owa\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\V15\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | append [| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" AND (Processes.process_name="cmd.exe" OR Processes.process_name="powershell.exe" OR Processes.process_name="net.exe" OR Processes.process_name="whoami.exe" OR Processes.process_name="ipconfig.exe" OR Processes.process_name="systeminfo.exe" OR Processes.process_name="procdump.exe" OR Processes.process_name="7z.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)] | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM ProxyLogon-style webshell drop
let _exchange_paths = dynamic([
    @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\",
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\",
    @"\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\",
    @"\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\"
]);
let _shell_extensions = dynamic([".aspx", ".ashx", ".asmx"]);
let _drops =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated", "FileRenamed", "FileModified")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"               // IIS worker process
    | where FolderPath has_any (_exchange_paths)
    | where FileName endswithif_any = true
    | extend ext = tolower(extract(@"(\.[a-zA-Z0-9]+)$", 1, FileName))
    | where ext in (_shell_extensions)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
              InitiatingProcessId, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, EventKind="WebshellWrite";
let _shells_spawned =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe",
                         "whoami.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe",
                         "procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","7z.exe","rar.exe","makecab.exe",
                         "nltest.exe","ntdsutil.exe","vssadmin.exe","wmic.exe")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
              FileName, ProcessCommandLine, FolderPath, SHA256,
              InitiatingProcessId, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, EventKind="IISChildShell";
union isfuzzy=true _drops, _shells_spawned
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM ProxyLogon-style webshell drop — Sentinel via ASIM
let _exchange_paths = dynamic([
    @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\",
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\",
    @"\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\",
    @"\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\"
]);
let _drops =
    ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | where ActingProcessName has "w3wp.exe"
    | where TargetFilePath has_any (_exchange_paths)
    | where TargetFileName endswith ".aspx" or TargetFileName endswith ".ashx" or TargetFileName endswith ".asmx"
    | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              TargetFileName, TargetFilePath, TargetFileSHA256,
              ActingProcessCommandLine, EventKind="WebshellWrite";
let _shells_spawned =
    ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where ParentProcessName has "w3wp.exe"
    | where TargetProcessName has_any ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe",
                                       "whoami.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe",
                                       "procdump.exe","7z.exe","rar.exe","nltest.exe",
                                       "ntdsutil.exe","vssadmin.exe","wmic.exe")
    | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              TargetProcessName, TargetProcessCommandLine,
              ParentProcessCommandLine, EventKind="IISChildShell";
union isfuzzy=true _drops, _shells_spawned
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) ProxyLogon Webshell Drop by IIS w3wp.exe
id: 7a3c2c4d-91b1-4f8b-9f67-silktyphoon0001
status: experimental
description: Detects the IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) writing .aspx/.ashx/.asmx files into Microsoft Exchange OWA/ECP/aspnet_client directories — the canonical Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM ProxyLogon webshell drop (CVE-2021-26855 et al., Operation Exchange Marauder).
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/06
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.g0125
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_process:
    Image|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  selection_extension:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.aspx'
      - '.ashx'
      - '.asmx'
  selection_path:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\'
      - '\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\'
      - '\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\'
      - '\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\'
      - '\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Microsoft Exchange cumulative updates / security hotfixes writing legitimate provider .aspx files (rare; correlate with KBxxxxxxx install events on the same host)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Pinned squarely on Silk Typhoon's signature initial-access chain. The combination of (a) IIS worker process w3wp.exe as parent, (b) write into Exchange virtual-directory paths, and (c) .aspx/.ashx/.asmx extension is the documented HAFNIUM webshell-drop fingerprint reported by Microsoft and Volexity in March 2021 and reused across subsequent Silk Typhoon Exchange/Citrix/Ivanti campaigns. The compan
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon service-principal credential addition / OAuth app cert injection (post-2024 cloud pivot)
ucs.description: Hunts Silk Typhoon's 2024+ tradecraft documented in Microsoft Threat Intelligence's March 2025 advisory: after stealing API keys or exploiting privileged-access-management / Entra Connect tooling at IT-supply-chain victims, the actor adds new client secrets or X.509 certificates to existing service principals / multi-tenant apps to maintain Graph access across customer tenants. The single high-fidelity primitive is an AAD audit 'Add service principal credentials' or 'Update application – Certificates and secrets management' from an unusual actor / IP.
ucs.splunk: `azure_audit` (operationName.value="Add service principal credentials." OR operationName.value="Update application – Certificates and secrets management." OR operationName.value="Update application" OR operationName.value="Add owner to service principal.") activityResult="Success" | rename initiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName AS actor_upn initiatedBy.user.ipAddress AS actor_ip initiatedBy.app.displayName AS actor_app properties.targetResources{}.displayName AS app_name properties.targetResources{}.id AS app_id | eval actor=coalesce(actor_upn, actor_app) | stats min(_time) AS firstTime max(_time) AS lastTime values(operationName.value) AS operations values(app_name) AS apps values(app_id) AS app_ids dc(app_id) AS apps_modified_count by actor actor_ip | where apps_modified_count >= 1 | lookup known_change_actors actor OUTPUT is_known | search NOT is_known=true | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon — addition of credentials/certs to service principals (cloud persistence)
let _silktyphoon_ops = dynamic([
    "Add service principal credentials.",
    "Update application – Certificates and secrets management.",
    "Update application",
    "Add owner to service principal.",
    "Add owner to application.",
    "Add delegated permission grant."
]);
CloudAppEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where Application == "Office 365"                       // AAD audit ingestion
| where ActionType in (_silktyphoon_ops)
| extend Raw = parse_json(RawEventData)
| extend TargetApp = tostring(Raw.Target_1.ID),
         InitiatorApp = tostring(Raw.ActorContextId),
         CredType = tostring(parse_json(tostring(AdditionalFields)).KeyType)
| where AccountType !in~ ("Application")                  // human / interactive actor
| join kind=leftouter (
    IdentityInfo
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | summarize arg_max(Timestamp, JobTitle, Department, IsAccountEnabled) by AccountObjectId
    | project AccountObjectId, JobTitle, Department, IsAccountEnabled
  ) on AccountObjectId
| where IsAccountEnabled != false
| project Timestamp, ActionType, AccountDisplayName, AccountObjectId,
          JobTitle, Department,
          IPAddress, CountryCode, City, ISP, IsAnonymousProxy,
          UserAgent, ActivityObjects, AdditionalFields
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon — credential/cert addition to AAD apps & service principals
let _silktyphoon_ops = dynamic([
    "Add service principal credentials",
    "Update application – Certificates and secrets management",
    "Update application",
    "Add owner to service principal",
    "Add owner to application",
    "Add delegated permission grant"
]);
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where OperationName in (_silktyphoon_ops)
| where Result == "success"
| extend Initiator = coalesce(
        tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.userPrincipalName),
        tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).app.displayName)
    ),
    InitIp = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.ipAddress),
    AppName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName),
    AppId   = tostring(TargetResources[0].id),
    ChangedProps = tostring(TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties)
| where ChangedProps has_any ("KeyDescription", "PasswordCredentials", "KeyCredentials")
| join kind=leftouter (
    SigninLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
    | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, IPAddress, tostring(parse_json(LocationDetails).countryOrRegion))
        by UserPrincipalName
  ) on $left.Initiator == $right.UserPrincipalName
| project TimeGenerated, OperationName, Initiator, InitIp, AppName, AppId,
          ChangedProps, AdditionalDetails, CorrelationId
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: Microsoft Threat Intelligence (MSTIC) explicitly attributes Silk Typhoon's 2024–2025 cloud pivot to credential addition on service principals as a primary persistence mechanism — the actor steals API keys / abuses PAM tooling at managed-service-provider victims, then drops new password/key credentials onto existing OAuth apps so that Graph tokens can be minted across downstream tenants. Generic 'n
